config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika hak individu dihormati dan tanggung jawab bersama dijalankan seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we have seen too many examples of “the greater good” being used to excuse abuse, discrimination, or government overreach. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in a vacuum; public safety, public health, and basic order sometimes require limits, but those limits should be narrow and carefully justified. To me, the burden should be on society to prove why a restriction is necessary, not on the person to prove why their rights deserve protection. If we flip that standard, it becomes too easy to trample minorities and unpopular voices whenever the majority claims a social benefit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk menempatkan hak individu di bawah begitu saja. Di Indonesia, kita sering melihat dalih “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan itu berbahaya karena mudah menyasar kelompok kecil atau suara yang tidak populer. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu praktis. Masyarakat yang kuat justru terbentuk saat negara melindungi hak per orang sambil mengatur kepentingan bersama dengan adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, history makes me wary of any vague appeal to “the common good” because it has often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government abuse against people with less power. That said, I do not think individual rights exist in a vacuum: public safety, health, and order can justify limits, but only when those limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If society gets to override rights too easily, then minority rights become whatever the majority feels like granting.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil, membungkam suara berbeda, atau membenarkan keputusan yang sebenarnya lebih menguntungkan pihak kuat. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya kalau alasannya jelas, perlu, dan proporsional, bukan karena mayoritas merasa itu paling praktis. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak per orang yang kuat, lalu kepentingan bersama diatur tanpa mengorbankan yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, I am deeply skeptical of any broad “common good” argument because history shows how easily it becomes a cover for censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power against unpopular people. Public safety and public health can justify limits, but only when those limits are narrow, necessary, and tightly accountable; otherwise, the majority can too easily redefine “society’s interests” to suit itself. To me, the real test of a just society is whether it protects individual rights even when doing so is inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
